queue.title: Best practices are trapped in your head and your AI has no idea ft. Harald Kirschner | Config 2026
queue.description: Your tone of voice, your component principles, your definition of "done"—this is the taste you've built over years. Right now it lives in your head, in scattered docs, or in places your tools can't see. Every AI session starts from scratch because that context is invisible. This talk shows how to connect those dots: building a shared knowledge layer so AI can help with specs, prototypes, and desig
queue.editorial_reason: Sharp framing of how design taste/best-practices get lost to AI agents unless externalized into structured context.

queue.title: Stop Checking for None Everywhere
queue.description:
🧱 Build software that lasts. Join the Software Design Mastery waiting list → https://arjan.codes/mastery.

'None' seems harmless at first. But once it starts spreading through your codebase, every function needs more checks, more validation, and more defensive programming.

In this video, I show several ways to avoid overusing 'None', including better defaults, fail-fast validation, explicit state
queue.editorial_reason: ArjanCodes gives concrete patterns to avoid None-checking sprawl, solid software craft content with real code.

Eddie Kim is the co-founder and Head of Technology at Gusto (W12), a payroll and HR platform serving over 500,000 small businesses in the U.S. that recently crossed $1 billion in annual revenue — with one in five new businesses started today becoming Gusto customers. 

Recently, Eddie built Gusto Cofounder, a new AI product that automates recurring business processes end-to-end, all triggered thro
queue.editorial_reason: YC talk on how Gusto built an internal AI co-founder tool; credible builder sharing specific product decisions.

queue.title: Stop Babysitting Your AI: Context Gathering & Code Review, Looped!
queue.description:

How it evolved and where it is going.

Join the BMAD Discord to shape what comes next: htt
queue.editorial_reason: Long-form walkthrough of a concrete agentic dev-review loop with an open-source contributor; detailed workflow.

queue.title: LGTM, Ship It: The AI Code Review Problem
queue.description: This episode tackles the growing pains of AI-assisted development, from the struggle of reviewing thousands of lines of agent-generated code to the mounting technical debt when teams merge PRs without meaningful human review. Scott and Wes also dig into local models, whether jujutsu really beats git, how freelancers should price work in the AI era, and getting your team on board with external libr
queue.editorial_reason: Syntax podcast tackles real problems of reviewing AI-generated code and tech debt, credible working developers.

queue.title: Cactus Needle - The 26M Function Calling Model
queue.description:
In this video, I look at Needle by the company Cactus. This is a 26 million parameter function calling model with a very unique architecture that is outperforming a lot of models much bigger than itself. 

Blog: https://cactuscompute.com/blog/needle
Demo:  https://huggingface.co/spaces/shreyask/needle-playground
Github: https://github.com/cactus-compute/needle 

Twitter: https://x.com/Sam_Wittevee
queue.editorial_reason: Sam Witteveen examines an unusually small function-calling model's architecture with real technical substance.

queue.title: In search of frontier AI at home
queue.description:
Analyzing a handful of local models that fit within a specific GPU memory ceiling, looking to learn how much compression impacts performance, how differently compressed models @ similar size compare, and how kv cache impacts attention in some cases.

DeepSeek V4 Flash: https://huggingface.co/deepseek-ai/DeepSeek

He’s spent five months figuring out which local models belong on
queue.editorial_reason: Detailed walkthrough of an ambitious multi-Mac-Studio local AI cluster setup, practical for serious builders.

pool.title: Beyond design systems: designing for robots and seniors ft. Anna Oh (Norbert Health) | Config 2026
pool.description: While AI reshapes the world, many who need it most are left behind. At Norbert Health, Anna Oh designs robotic AI for seniors with dementia and limited mobility in nursing homes. Working directly with real patients and nurses, Oh and her team built human systems that help robots listen, respond, and comfort.This talk explores what patients taught Anna about embodied AI, and why design isn’t just a
pool.editorial_reason: Real-world HCI case study designing robots for seniors with dementia; specific, credible, humane engineering insight.

pool.title: Prototyping for the physical world ft. Frederik Ueberschär & Sam Thorne | Config 2026
pool.description: What if Figma’s canvas could extend into the physical world? At Electrolux, we use Figma to design the next generation of home appliances. This talk shares how we bridged the digital-physical gap by building "Figma Displays" with off-the-shelf electronics to live-stream prototypes onto production-accurate screens. Learn how this new process shrunk our feedback loop from six months to just two minu
pool.editorial_reason: Electrolux team's unusual physical-prototyping workflow bridging Figma to hardware displays, specific and novel.

pool.title: From handoffs to upstream ft. Frederick Andersen (EDL) | Config 2026
pool.description: A year ago, EDL made a bet. Not on a tool or a trend, but on a person and a different idea of what a design studio could own. Today, EDL pushes to clients' Git repos, runs their design systems in code, and sits upstream of their engineering teams. This wasn't accidental. It was intentional. This is the full story: the moment the team saw the opportunity, the rituals that made it real, the metrics
pool.editorial_reason: Design studio's real transition to owning code/Git upstream of engineering — concrete workflow shift, not hype.

pool.title: Human methods, new material: AI for the classroom ft. Mubarak Marafa & Wendy Liao | Config 2026
pool.description: How do you build with AI for a world as human, messy, and high-stakes as the classroom? This talk takes you inside the 0–1 journey of Goodnotes Classroom: from real teacher workarounds and classroom observations to fast prototypes, co-creation sessions, and product decisions shaped by educators themselves. We’ll share how human methods became the foundation for designing with AI as a new material,
pool.editorial_reason: Detailed 0-to-1 case study designing AI classroom tools with real teacher research, not generic AI hype.

pool.title: Be the director: how stage craft informs product craft ft. Kim Beverett (OpenAI) | Config 2026
pool.description:
The screen is the stage, and the user is the audience. In this talk, we’ll explore how to think like a stage director by making intentional choices, defining the non-negotiables in your script, and finding your product’s spine. And why the show must go on.
____________________________________________________

Find us on ⬇️
X (formerly Twitter): https://x.com/figma
Instagram: https://www.instagram.
pool.editorial_reason: OpenAI designer's stagecraft framing for product design decisions offers a genuinely fresh mental model.

pool.title: The metacognitive design loop ft. Jenny Au & Mike Green | Config 2026
pool.description: Learn how Epsilon's Product Design Team transformed front-end development by shifting the QA process to the very beginning of the design phase. By combining a Metacognitive Design Loop with Figma's Code Connect and MCP with their CORE UI component library, the team has revolutionized product delivery and achieved unprecedented development velocity—moving from design to production faster than ever
pool.editorial_reason: Concrete case study shifting QA earlier via Figma MCP/Code Connect in a real design-to-dev pipeline.

pool.title: Protoypes, failures, and unexpected breakthroughs ft. Noah Finer (Figma) | Config 2026
pool.description:
Noah spends many days at Figma prototyping janky interactions, and other days perfecting editor microinteractions so subtle they almost go unnoticed. How do these two engineering mindsets interact? And how have they shaped the way Noah builds experiences like Figma Slides, FigPals, and interactive transit personality tests?
____________________________________________________

Find us on ⬇️
X (for
pool.editorial_reason: Figma engineer reflects candidly on prototyping failures and craft tradeoffs behind shipped features.

pool.title: Scooter, screen and duct-tape: from hack to habit ft. Unni K. Manikoth & Harish Kumar | Config 2026
pool.description:
What if your next great design tool doesn’t exist yet? At Ather Energy, designers built their own using Arduinos, iPads, and Figma. This talk unpacks the ways of going outside the design loop, how hacks become habits, and how scrappy experimentation helps teams validate ideas, influence stakeholders, and build magical real-world experiences faster.
_________________________________________________
pool.editorial_reason: Scrappy DIY hardware+Figma prototyping story showing how hacks become durable design tools at a real company.

In this workshop we will dig into Figma’s vector tools for creating scalable vector icons for interfaces. We will cover concepts like shapes, boolean operations, the pen tool, Figma Draw features, as well as best practices, shortcuts, and approaches.
